title: delphinium parishii subsp. pallidum
intro: Delphinium parishii subsp. pallidum, commonly known as pale larkspur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains delphinine, a toxic alkaloid with antimicrobial and antitumor properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, pale larkspur is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
